title: Kalimeris altaica
intro: Kalimeris altaica, also known as Kalimeris, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in relieving stress, improving sleep quality, and boosting the immune system. Kalimeris altaica contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, saponins, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine and is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or decoction to harness its benefits, although further research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.
taxonomy: Kalimeris altaica is classified as a species within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. uses: Kalimeris altaica is known for its potential in traditional medicine, particularly in Asian cultures. Its root and leaves are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is also used to relieve headaches, coughs, and sore throats.
